title: Clarify Inc.
author: Aberdeen Group
abstract: Aberdeen Group evaluates Clarify Inc., a San Jose-based leader in customer interaction software (CIS) covering customer service, field service, quality assurance, helpdesk, and sales force automation. The study documents Clarify's integrated best-of-breed strategy, 99% uptime reliability, 100%+ annual revenue growth, linchpin accounts at Microsoft, Cisco, HP, MCI, and Sprint, and the Metropolis Software acquisition providing SFA and mobile computing capabilities. Aberdeen concludes Clarify is 'a CIS winner' positioned to dominate the rapidly consolidating CIS market.
importance_rationale: Clarify was a leading CRM/CIS vendor in 1996 with blue-chip anchor accounts (Microsoft, Cisco, HP, MCI); the study documents the early CRM market consolidation that culminated in Nortel's $2.1B acquisition of Clarify in 1999 — one of the most notable enterprise software deals of the era.
relevance_rationale: The CIS/CRM market analysis framework, metadata-based customization architecture, and integrated best-of-breed strategy remain analytically useful; specific products (ClearSupport, ClearSales) are obsolete but the customer success management principles and 'linchpin account' strategy remain relevant.
prescience_rationale: Aberdeen correctly predicted Clarify's continued rapid growth and strong position in IT/telecom markets; Clarify was acquired by Nortel for $2.1B in 1999, validating the 'CIS winner' assessment. The prediction of CIS market consolidation and Aberdeen's specific identification of Clarify as a leader proved accurate.
